text.title: Constitution gates inside plan.md
text.summary: plan.md must record evidence that each constitution principle was honored or explicitly waived; missing evidence blocks task generation.

section.title: Gate-as-evidence
section.p: Spec-kit's constitution.md ships nine immutable principles (library-first, test-first, simplicity gate, anti-abstraction, integration-first testing, etc.). The plan template embeds them as a checklist; each item must carry a one-sentence justification or an explicit waiver before `/speckit.tasks` will run. The constitution moves "good practice" from advice into machine-checked precondition.

rule.statement: plan.md MUST contain a "Constitution Gates" section enumerating each principle with one of three values: `pass` (with one-sentence evidence), `waived` (with reviewer name and link to the waiver issue), or `blocked` (which halts the pipeline). The agent MUST NOT generate tasks.md while any gate is `blocked`. Waivers are only valid if signed by a CODEOWNER and reference an issue ID.
rule.rationale: Without recorded evidence, the gate degenerates into a comment the LLM ignores. By forcing a value plus citation per principle, the plan becomes auditable and the cost of skipping a principle (a CODEOWNER review) is now non-zero — which is what makes the gate behavioural.

example.code:
## Constitution Gates
| Principle | Status | Evidence | |-----------|--------|----------| | Library-first | pass | New auth code lives in `libs/auth/`, not `apps/web/views/`. | | Test-first | pass | T-001..T-004 are pytest cases; T-005+ depend on them. | | Simplicity gate | waived | #4112 — multi-tenant requires extra layer; reviewer @ruslan. | | Anti-abstraction | pass | Direct Authlib calls; no in-house wrapper. | | Integration-first testing | pass | T-006 spins up real Google sandbox via VCR cassette. |
example.note: Every row has a value and a citation; the pipeline can proceed.
Aggregate pass with no per-principle evidence — equivalent to no gate.

antipattern.description: Agent waives a gate by writing "waived" without linking a CODEOWNER-signed issue ID, then generates tasks.md anyway.
antipattern.reason: Unsigned waivers re-introduce silent assumptions; the next reviewer cannot tell if the bypass was approved or invented by the model.
